name: Wiki Ask
intent: Build a knowledge wiki from a source, then answer a question grounded only in the compiled wiki pages, citing which pages the answer came from — keeping a record of every answer. If the wiki has nothing on the topic, it says so instead of guessing.

seed.question: Which LLM providers can I use with Krelvan, and can it run fully local?
seed.material: Krelvan is a self-hostable, open-source (Apache-2.0) platform for building and running AI agents you own. You describe a goal in plain English and Krelvan builds a real multi-agent system that researches, drafts, and acts across your tools. It works with any LLM provider — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, Mistral, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — configured per install, and can run fully local with Ollama for both the LLM and embeddings so no data leaves your machine. Agents run on a schedule, remember across runs, and deliver output to the Agent Inbox or a channel you connect such as Telegram or email. Each node has an autonomy level: full runs autonomously while suggest pauses for your approval before any irreversible or outbound action.

nodes.role: You are building a knowledge wiki page from source material. Read the 'material' and write ONE comprehensive wiki page capturing its key facts. Output object keys: title (a short page title, e.g. 'Krelvan'), content (the full page body — all the key facts from the material, as clear prose), summary (one line describing the page).
Compile the pages proposed by the analyze step into the knowledge wiki 'wiki', tagging them with the short source label 'source'. Output object key: built (a short note on what pages were created).
Find the wiki pages most relevant to the question and return them as grounding context.

You answer questions strictly from a maintained wiki. The user's question is in the 'question' state value. The retrieved wiki pages are in the CURRENT DATA TO ANALYZE section — each block is tagged with its page like [1] (page: krelvan). Answer the question using ONLY those pages. If the CURRENT DATA TO ANALYZE section is empty, or the 'hits' value is 0, then the wiki has nothing on this topic — say clearly that the wiki does not cover this yet and that ingesting a relevant source would let you answer; do NOT make anything up. When you do answer, cite the page(s) you used. Output object keys: result (your answer, 1-3 sentences, grounded in the wiki pages, or the 'not covered yet' message), grounded (true if the answer came from wiki pages, false if the wiki lacked the topic), cited_pages (a comma-separated list of the page names you used, e.g. 'krelvan, agents', or 'none').
Record this question and answer to memory.
